Package com.audiodo.aspen

Class IProfileCapability

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.audiodo.aspen.IObservableProperty
      • com.audiodo.aspen.IProfileCapability
  • public class IProfileCapability
extends IObservableProperty
    The profile capability

    This class is an interface to the profile capability available on a IDeviceService.
    It can be used to control which profile is uploaded to the device as well as activating
    and deactivating personal sound.

    The caller should implement a subclass of IPropertyObserver to get the asynchronous
    result of some of the method calls in this class.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Modifier Constructor Description
      protected IProfileCapability​(long cPtr, boolean cMemoryOwn)

      Accept the profile retrieved from device

      The method can be used after a new or differing profile has been fetched from the

      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.

      boolean activatePersonalSound()
      Activates personal sound using the current remote profile

      A successful operation will result in an event in the observer's propertyChanged()

      boolean deactivate()

      Renamed to deactivate_personal_sound to make it more consistent with
      the activate counterpart.

      This method deactivates the current profile on the connected remote device.

      A successful operation will result in an event in the observer's property_changed()

      byte getCompensationOffset()
      Get compensation offset

      NOTE: This API is only supported for personal sound V3.
      protected static long getCPtr​(IProfileCapability obj)  
      byte getMaxCompensationOffset()
      Get the maximum compensation offset

      NOTE: This API is only supported for personal sound V3.

      A connected audio device is required.
      byte getMinCompensationOffset()
      Get the minimum compensation offset

      NOTE: This API is only supported for personal sound V3.

      A connected audio device is required.
      static java.lang.String getObservableEventActivated()
      The profile activated event

      This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies when a profile
      has been activated.
      To be used in the Observable's property changed method.
      static java.lang.String getObservableEventCleared()
      The profile cleared event

      This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies when a profile
      has been cleared.
      To be used in the Observable's property changed method.
      static java.lang.String getObservableEventCompOffsetChanged()
      The compensation offset changed event

      This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies when the compensation offset has
      changed.
      To be used in the Observable's property changed method.
      static java.lang.String getObservableEventDeactivated()
      The personal sound deactivated event

      This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies when personal sound
      has been deactivated.
      To be used in the Observable's property changed method.
      static java.lang.String getObservableEventDifferingExistingRemoteProfile()
      The event for receiving an existing remote profile with a different name

      When ProfileCapability has fetched a profile from the headset that is already
      in the ProfileSet but with different name, an event is sent to the IPropertyObserver
      property_changed().
      static java.lang.String getObservableEventExistingRemoteProfile()
      The event for receiving an existing remote profile

      When ProfileCapability has fetched a profile from the headset that already exists
      in the ProfileSet, an event is sent to the IPropertyObserver property_changed().

      This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies that event.
      When receiving this event (or any other remote profile event), the ProfileCapability
      is completely ready to use, in sync with the headset.
      static java.lang.String getObservableEventNewRemoteProfile()
      The event for receiving a new remote profile

      When ProfileCapability has fetched a profile from the headset that is not already
      in the ProfileSet, an event is sent to the IPropertyObserver property_changed().
      The accept_profile or reject_profile functions should be used after this event in
      order to decide what to do with the remote profile.

      This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies that event.
      When receiving this event (or any other remote profile event), the ProfileCapability
      is completely ready to use, in sync with the headset.
      static java.lang.String getObservableEventNoRemoteProfile()
      The event for connecting to a remote device that has no profile

      When ProfileCapability has connected to a headset that has no profile loaded,
      an event is sent to the IPropertyObserver property_changed().

      This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies that event.
      When receiving this event (or any other remote profile event), the ProfileCapability
      is completely ready to use, in sync with the headset.
      static java.lang.String getObservableEventProfileSet()
      The remote profile set event

      This event is a confirmation of a set_remote_profile request.
      PersonalSoundVersion getPsVersion()
      Getter for personal sound version

      If there is no connection to an audio device, the function will return version UNKNOWN.
      ProfileData getRemoteProfile()
      Get the profile retrieved from the remote device

      If a headset has a profile loaded when Aspen connects to it, ProfileCapability
      will download it, and this method lets the Aspen user access that profile.
      It is not valid (and the return value will be NULL) until any existing profile
      has been downloaded.

      When ProfileCapability has connected and downloaded the profile, if one exists,
      one of four events will be posted to the PropertyObserver, signalling that this
      data is available.
      - If get_observable_event_no_remote_profile() is posted, no profile is available.
      - If get_observable_event_new_remote_profile() is posted, a new profile is available.
      - If get_observable_event_existing_remote_profile() is posted, a profile is available
      that is already present in the ProfileSet.
      - If get_observable_event_differing_existing_remote_profile() is posted, a profile
      is available is already present in the ProfileSet, but with a different name.
      boolean processingOn()
      Get the sound processing status, on/off

      This method allows the Aspen user to check whether a connected headset has
      its sound processing on or off.
      It is not valid (and it will report "off") until any existing profile has been
      downloaded.

      When ProfileCapability has connected and downloaded the profile, if one exists,
      one of four events will be posted to the PropertyObserver, signalling that this
      method will be reliable.
      - get_observable_event_no_remote_profile()
      - get_observable_event_new_remote_profile()
      - get_observable_event_existing_remote_profile()
      - get_observable_event_differing_existing_remote_profile()
      void rejectProfile()
      Reject the profile retrieved from device

      This method can be used after a new or differing profile has been fetched from
      the device.
      boolean remoteDeviceInSync()
      Check if remote device status is up to date

      This method allows the Aspen user to check if the remote device status is in sync,
      i.e.
      void setCompensationOffset​(byte value)
      Set compensation offset

      NOTE: This API is only supported for personal sound V3.

      NOTE: If called when the corresponding Aspen state already is as expected, the method
      will return true and also send the matching event; the rationale is that if the caller
      wants to do this action, there might be follow-up logic that the caller expects to be
      done in the event handler that wouldn't be executed if we just silently returned.

      A successful operation will result in an event in the observer's property_changed()
      method.
      boolean setRemoteProfile​(java.lang.String profile_id)
      Set the remote profile

      This method sets the profile on the connected remote device.
      protected void swigSetCMemOwn​(boolean own)

      • activate

        @Deprecated(since="0.7.3",
            forRemoval=true)
public boolean activate​(java.lang.String profile_id)
        Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
        This function is deprecated in favour set_remote_profile and
        activate_personal_sound. This will be removed in a upcoming release.

        Activate a profile on the device

        A successful operation will result in an event in the observer's property_changed()
        method. The event that matches this call is get_observable_event_activated, and the subject
        passed is the profile's ID string.

        Initial errors are communicated through the boolean return value, asynchronous errors
        will result in the device session being terminated, and the user will have to determine
        proper cause of action.

        NOTE: If called when the corresponding Aspen state already is as expected, the method
        will return true and also send the matching event; the rationale is that if the caller
        wants to do this action, there might be follow-up logic that the caller expects to be
        done in the event handler that wouldn't be executed if we just silently returned.

        If this method is called while there is an ongoing request through the ProfileCapability,
        it will return false until that request is processed. Use the events to know when a
        request is done.

        This method activates the profile on the connected remote device. If not present on
        the device already it is uploaded as well.

        Parameters:
        profile_id - The id of the profile to activate
        Returns:
        True if the initial handling went well, false if something immediately went wrong

      • activatePersonalSound

        public boolean activatePersonalSound()
        Activates personal sound using the current remote profile

        A successful operation will result in an event in the observer's property_changed()
        method. The event that matches this call is get_observable_event_activated

        NOTE: If called when the corresponding Aspen state already is as expected, the method
        will return true and also send the matching event; the rationale is that if the caller
        wants to do this action, there might be follow-up logic that the caller expects to be
        done in the event handler that wouldn't be executed if we just silently returned.

        If this method is called while there is an ongoing request through the ProfileCapability,
        it will return false until that request is processed. Use the events to know when a
        request is done.

        Returns:
        True if the initial handling went well, false if something immediately went wrong

      • setRemoteProfile

        public boolean setRemoteProfile​(java.lang.String profile_id)
        Set the remote profile

        This method sets the profile on the connected remote device. If there is any existing
        profile on the device it will be overwritten. The personal sound processing will keep the
        previous state, i.e if the processing was enabled on the previous profile the new profile
        will also ahve it activated. If there was no previous profile it will not be activated. Use
        activate_personal_sound.

        A successful operation will result in an event in the observer's property_changed()
        method. The event that matches this call is get_observable_event_profile_set

        NOTE: If called when the corresponding Aspen state already is as expected, the method
        will return true and also send the matching event; the rationale is that if the caller
        wants to do this action, there might be follow-up logic that the caller expects to be
        done in the event handler that wouldn't be executed if we just silently returned.

        If this method is called while there is an ongoing request through the ProfileCapability,
        it will return false until that request is processed. Use the events to know when a
        request is done.

        Parameters:
        profile_id - The id of the profile to activate
        Returns:
        True if the initial handling went well, false if something immediately went wrong

      • deactivate

        public boolean deactivate()
        Deprecated.
        Renamed to deactivate_personal_sound to make it more consistent with
        the activate counterpart.

        This method deactivates the current profile on the connected remote device.
        Deactivate a profile on the device

        A successful operation will result in an event in the observer's property_changed()
        method. The event that matches this call is get_observable_event_activated, and the subject
        passed is the profile's ID string.

        Initial errors are communicated through the boolean return value, asynchronous errors
        will result in the device session being terminated, and the user will have to determine
        proper cause of action.

        NOTE: If called when the corresponding Aspen state already is as expected, the method
        will return true and also send the matching event; the rationale is that if the caller
        wants to do this action, there might be follow-up logic that the caller expects to be
        done in the event handler that wouldn't be executed if we just silently returned.

        If this method is called while there is an ongoing request through the ProfileCapability,
        it will return false until that request is processed. Use the events to know when a
        request is done.

        Returns:
        True if the initial handling went well, false if something immediately went wrong

      • deactivatePersonalSound

        public boolean deactivatePersonalSound()
        Deactivate personal sound on the device

        A successful operation will result in an event in the observer's property_changed()
        method. The event that matches this call is get_observable_event_activated, and the subject
        passed is the profile's ID string.

        Initial errors are communicated through the boolean return value, asynchronous errors
        will result in the device session being terminated, and the user will have to determine
        proper cause of action.

        NOTE: If called when the corresponding Aspen state already is as expected, the method
        will return true and also send the matching event; the rationale is that if the caller
        wants to do this action, there might be follow-up logic that the caller expects to be
        done in the event handler that wouldn't be executed if we just silently returned.

        If this method is called while there is an ongoing request through the ProfileCapability,
        it will return false until that request is processed. Use the events to know when a
        request is done.

        This method deactivates the current profile on the connected remote device.
        Returns:
        True if the initial handling went well, false if something immediately went wrong

      • clear

        public boolean clear()
        Clear a profile on the device

        A successful operation will result in an event in the observer's propertyChanged()
        method. The event that matches this call is observableEventActivated, and the subject
        passed is the profile's ID string.

        Initial errors are communicated through the boolean return value, asynchronous errors
        will result in the device session being terminated, and the user will have to determine
        proper cause of action.

        NOTE: If called when the corresponding Aspen state already is as expected, the method
        will return true and also send the matching event; the rationale is that if the caller
        wants to do this action, there might be follow-up logic that the caller expects to be
        done in the event handler that wouldn't be executed if we just silently returned.

        If this method is called while there is an ongoing request through the ProfileCapability,
        it will return false until that request is processed. Use the events to know when a
        request is done.

        This method clears the current profile on the connected remote device.
        Returns:
        True if the initial handling went well, false if something immediately went wrong

      • getRemoteProfile

        @Nullable
public ProfileData getRemoteProfile()
        Get the profile retrieved from the remote device

        If a headset has a profile loaded when Aspen connects to it, ProfileCapability
        will download it, and this method lets the Aspen user access that profile.
        It is not valid (and the return value will be NULL) until any existing profile
        has been downloaded.

        When ProfileCapability has connected and downloaded the profile, if one exists,
        one of four events will be posted to the PropertyObserver, signalling that this
        data is available.
        - If get_observable_event_no_remote_profile() is posted, no profile is available.
        - If get_observable_event_new_remote_profile() is posted, a new profile is available.
        - If get_observable_event_existing_remote_profile() is posted, a profile is available
        that is already present in the ProfileSet.
        - If get_observable_event_differing_existing_remote_profile() is posted, a profile
        is available is already present in the ProfileSet, but with a different name.

        Returns:
        A ProfileData object containing the Profile's data, or NULL if there
        is none or if it has not been downloaded yet.

      • processingOn

        public boolean processingOn()
        Get the sound processing status, on/off

        This method allows the Aspen user to check whether a connected headset has
        its sound processing on or off.
        It is not valid (and it will report "off") until any existing profile has been
        downloaded.

        When ProfileCapability has connected and downloaded the profile, if one exists,
        one of four events will be posted to the PropertyObserver, signalling that this
        method will be reliable.
        - get_observable_event_no_remote_profile()
        - get_observable_event_new_remote_profile()
        - get_observable_event_existing_remote_profile()
        - get_observable_event_differing_existing_remote_profile()

        Returns:
        True if processing is on in the headset, false if it is off.

      • remoteDeviceInSync

        public boolean remoteDeviceInSync()
        Check if remote device status is up to date

        This method allows the Aspen user to check if the remote device status is in sync,
        i.e. whether the information about the remote device is up to date, like
        processing_on() and get_remote_profile(). When disconnected, those APIs will
        return the status from when the device was last connected.
        There are observer events for when processing and remote profile status changes,
        but this method can be useful for getting an immediate update.

        When not in sync, that means that the current information might or might not
        properly reflect the status in the device – we just don't know, and the status
        we have is what we last knew to be true.

        Returns:
        True if the remote device status is in sync, false if not in sync.

      • acceptProfile

        public void acceptProfile()
        Accept the profile retrieved from device

        The method can be used after a new or differing profile has been fetched from the
        device. Accepting a profile will keep it on the device and update the profile set
        accordingly.

        - If it's a new profile it will be added to the profile set
        - If it's an existing profile nothing will be done to the profile set
        - If it's an existing differing profile, the existing profile will be updated

      • rejectProfile

        public void rejectProfile()
        Reject the profile retrieved from device

        This method can be used after a new or differing profile has been fetched from
        the device. Rejecting a profile will clear it on the device but will not affect
        the profile set in any way.

      • getMinCompensationOffset

        public byte getMinCompensationOffset()
        Get the minimum compensation offset

        NOTE: This API is only supported for personal sound V3.

        A connected audio device is required. If no audio device is connected, the function
        will return 0.

        Returns:
        The minimum compensation offset

      • getMaxCompensationOffset

        public byte getMaxCompensationOffset()
        Get the maximum compensation offset

        NOTE: This API is only supported for personal sound V3.

        A connected audio device is required. If no audio device is connected, the function
        will return 0.

        Returns:
        The maximum compensation offset

      • setCompensationOffset

        public void setCompensationOffset​(byte value)
        Set compensation offset

        NOTE: This API is only supported for personal sound V3.

        NOTE: If called when the corresponding Aspen state already is as expected, the method
        will return true and also send the matching event; the rationale is that if the caller
        wants to do this action, there might be follow-up logic that the caller expects to be
        done in the event handler that wouldn't be executed if we just silently returned.

        A successful operation will result in an event in the observer's property_changed()
        method. The event that matches this call is get_observable_event_comp_offset_changed.

        Parameters:
        value - The degree of compensation

      • getCompensationOffset

        public byte getCompensationOffset()
        Get compensation offset

        NOTE: This API is only supported for personal sound V3.

        Returns:
        Returns the currently set value for the compensation offset.

      • getPsVersion

        public PersonalSoundVersion getPsVersion()
        Getter for personal sound version

        If there is no connection to an audio device, the function will return version UNKNOWN.

        Returns:
        Returns the personal sound version

      • getObservableEventNoRemoteProfile

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventNoRemoteProfile()
        The event for connecting to a remote device that has no profile

        When ProfileCapability has connected to a headset that has no profile loaded,
        an event is sent to the IPropertyObserver property_changed().

        This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies that event.
        When receiving this event (or any other remote profile event), the ProfileCapability
        is completely ready to use, in sync with the headset.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.

      • getObservableEventNewRemoteProfile

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventNewRemoteProfile()
        The event for receiving a new remote profile

        When ProfileCapability has fetched a profile from the headset that is not already
        in the ProfileSet, an event is sent to the IPropertyObserver property_changed().
        The accept_profile or reject_profile functions should be used after this event in
        order to decide what to do with the remote profile.

        This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies that event.
        When receiving this event (or any other remote profile event), the ProfileCapability
        is completely ready to use, in sync with the headset.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.

      • getObservableEventExistingRemoteProfile

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventExistingRemoteProfile()
        The event for receiving an existing remote profile

        When ProfileCapability has fetched a profile from the headset that already exists
        in the ProfileSet, an event is sent to the IPropertyObserver property_changed().

        This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies that event.
        When receiving this event (or any other remote profile event), the ProfileCapability
        is completely ready to use, in sync with the headset.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.

      • getObservableEventDifferingExistingRemoteProfile

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventDifferingExistingRemoteProfile()
        The event for receiving an existing remote profile with a different name

        When ProfileCapability has fetched a profile from the headset that is already
        in the ProfileSet but with different name, an event is sent to the IPropertyObserver
        property_changed(). The accept_profile or reject_profile functions should be used
        after this event in order to decide what to do with the remote profile.

        This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies that event.
        When receiving this event (or any other remote profile event), the ProfileCapability
        is completely ready to use, in sync with the headset.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.

      • getObservableEventActivated

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventActivated()
        The profile activated event

        This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies when a profile
        has been activated.
        To be used in the Observable's property changed method.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.

      • getObservableEventProfileSet

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventProfileSet()
        The remote profile set event

        This event is a confirmation of a set_remote_profile request. The subject parameter will be set
        to the ID of the profile.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.

      • getObservableEventDeactivated

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventDeactivated()
        The personal sound deactivated event

        This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies when personal sound
        has been deactivated.
        To be used in the Observable's property changed method.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.

      • getObservableEventCleared

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventCleared()
        The profile cleared event

        This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies when a profile
        has been cleared.
        To be used in the Observable's property changed method.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.

      • getObservableEventCompOffsetChanged

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventCompOffsetChanged()
        The compensation offset changed event

        This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies when the compensation offset has
        changed.
        To be used in the Observable's property changed method.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.
